config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before individual rights, because once the state or the majority is allowed to override people too easily, abuse becomes much more likely. Individual rights are the basic protection that keeps society fair, especially for minorities and unpopular voices. From my perspective, a good society protects the common good without treating the individual as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that is exactly how abuse gets justified in the name of “the greater good.” Coming from a Chinese perspective, I understand why people emphasize harmony, stability, and collective welfare, but I also know how easily those values can be used to silence ordinary people when their rights are inconvenient. A society is only truly strong if it can protect both the common good and the dignity of each person, especially when the majority or the authorities are tempted to ignore them. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then society may become orderly, but not necessarily just.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not get blanket priority over the individual, because that opens the door to governments and majorities deciding whose rights matter less. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of gotong royong and social harmony, but I do not think those values should be used to justify silencing dissent, limiting due process, or sacrificing minorities for convenience. The common good matters, but it is not truly good if it is built by ignoring basic individual rights. In practice, the healthiest balance is to protect individual dignity first, then ask how society can be served without trampling it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not have blanket priority over the rights of the individual, because once that principle is accepted, it becomes very easy to excuse abuse in the name of stability or the greater good. From a Chinese perspective, I do value social harmony and collective responsibility, but I have also seen how those ideas can be used to pressure people into silence or make their suffering seem acceptable. A society is not truly healthy if it only looks orderly on the surface while ordinary people have no real protection when the majority or the authorities decide their rights are inconvenient. The common good matters, but it should be built on respect for individual dignity, not on sacrificing it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual, because that mindset too easily turns into excuses for abuse. From an Indonesian perspective, I value gotong royong and social harmony, but I do not believe those values justify silencing dissent, weakening due process, or treating minorities as expendable for convenience. The common good is important, but it is not genuinely good if it is built by ignoring basic rights. In practice, society is strongest when individual dignity is protected first and public interests are pursued within that limit.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not take priority over the rights of the individual by default, because that is exactly how people get told to endure injustice for the sake of order. From my experience as someone from China, I understand the appeal of harmony, stability, and collective responsibility, but I also know those ideas can be used to pressure ordinary people into silence when they need protection most. A society may look strong when everyone is obedient, but it is not truly legitimate if individuals have no real shield against the majority or the authorities. The common good matters, but it should be pursued through rights, not by treating rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
